ogTitle: McMahons Creek vs McMillans: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in McMahons Creek and McMillans.
heading: McMahons Creek vs McMillans — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between McMahons Creek, Victoria and McMillans, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: McMillans has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to McMahons Creek (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is McMahons Creek or McMillans safer from flooding?
faq.answer: McMahons Creek has a flood risk score of 29/100, while McMillans scores 40/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: McMahons Creek scores 51/100 for bushfire risk. McMillans scores 28/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in McMahons Creek or McMillans?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. McMillans has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
